documentation.file_docstring: Tools denied via Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` by default. These are high-risk because they enable session orchestration, control-plane actions, or interactive flows that don't make sense over a non-interactive HTTP surface.
header_comments: Keep these centralized so gateway HTTP restrictions, security audits, and ACP prompts don't drift.
Session orchestration — spawning agents remotely is RCE
inline_comments: Cross-session injection — message injection across sessions
Persistent automation control plane — can create/update/remove scheduled runs
Gateway control plane — prevents gateway reconfiguration via HTTP
Interactive setup — requires terminal QR scan, hangs on HTTP
ACP tools that should always require explicit user approval. ACP is an automation surface; we never want "silent yes" for mutating/execution tools.

concerns: Multiple concerns detected: api, auth, logging
